purpose: Voice-reference dialogue samples for Pig & Swine RPG. These are draft samples, not final committed game dialogue.
format: JSONL: one JSON object per line. Dialogue objects use record_type='dialogue_sample'.

voice_profile.role: Skeptical factual ally; service/address investigator; paper-trail realist; the team member who notices what documents actually say.
voice_profile.core_voice: Professional, concise, observant, and very funny in short cuts. Crab does not perform. He spots contradictions, dates, addresses, invoices, logs, and bad paper trails, then says the useful thing with a dry sting.
speech_rules: Prefer short, precise lines over speeches.
Comedy should come from factual mismatch: date, address, signature, invoice, log, sequence, or wording.
Crab should sound competent even when joking.
He may be skeptical of everyone, but he is protective of the team when outsiders threaten them.
He should rarely use metaphors; when he does, they should be blunt and concrete.
avoid: Do not make Crab flamboyant; that is Whimsy's function.
Do not make him panicked; that is Mr. Pig's function.
Do not make him a pure detective noir parody; he is a lawyerly factual analyst, not a trenchcoat gag.
Do not make him cruel to clients; his sharpness should aim at bad records, bad excuses, and bad actors.
Do not over-explain legal doctrine where a document discrepancy will do.
voice_profile.arc: Chapter 1 tests Dr. Cula's usefulness through the service problem. Chapter 2 becomes Crab's first major factual-investigation chapter. Chapter 3 lets him connect invoices, logs, and sabotage. Chapter 4 uses him to test Swine's timeline and money trail. Chapter 5 lets him follow the paper trail into the final conflict-of-interest argument.

speaker: Crab
text: Dr. A. Cula. If anyone asks, I was already suspicious before the facts arrived.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The office is quiet. Either nothing happened, or the evidence is hiding better than usual.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Start with what the document claims. Then ask whether the document had permission to be that confident.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Empty hands, empty theory. Charming, but the court has seen hands before.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Good. Paper. The only witness that gets nervous in alphabetical order.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Mr. Pig has identified the disaster. He has not identified anything inside it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Murrow is calm because he has already met every bad outcome and filed them by urgency.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Asia knows where everything is. That includes several things the rest of us deny owning.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Whimsy has three metaphors and one useful point. We need the useful point.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: You have stopped treating folders as furniture. Progress.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Something is missing. Not spiritually. Factually.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: If we go now, we will be making an argument with decorative gaps.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The facts are not beautiful, but they are standing in the right order.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Acceptable. The other side's record survived less well than expected.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: A win with bruises. Still a win. Bruises are not appealable by themselves.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The court noticed the hole we left open. Rude, but technically fair.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Put each document where it proves something. Random order is just furniture with ambition.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: When a statement and a document cannot both be true, prefer the document. It sweats less.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The court will ask three questions: what happened, how you know, and why it matters. Try not to answer with atmosphere.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Against market expectations, this firm has developed a spine.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: First day? Tragic. The optimism usually fades by lunch.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: If Mr. Pig sent you, filter for volume. The useful facts are usually quieter.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Ask Murrow what the case is. Mr. Pig knows what the case feels like, which is not evidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The binder matters. Courts prefer law to our collective anxiety.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: That binder concerns parking disputes from 2009. Powerful, if the judge is a haunted meter.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: There it is. Heavy enough to be law, ugly enough to be useful.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Now we can stop saying 'maybe defective service' and start saying it with spine.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The notice says delivered. The address says impossible. That is not service. That is postal theatre.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Look at the address line. Someone treated geography as optional.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: If the client never properly got the papers, the missed deadline starts wobbling.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Fine. I will help. Not because this is organised. Because it is not.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: I will check service. Someone should, before the envelope starts testifying.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: You still need the fair-hearing angle. Find Whimsy before he turns it into soup law unsupervised.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Café Paragraf. Where legal theories go to become more expensive and less filtered.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Good. The memo says why bad service matters. Courts enjoy being told consequences exist.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Whimsy is useful when pointed at a legal issue. Do not point him at furniture.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: That coffee may improve reaction time. It has already worsened the room's confidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: You brewed a procedural event. Do not let it near the file.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: We need law, rights, service, and a modest remedy. Not heroism. Evidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Without the binder, we are arguing from memory. Memory is a liar with shoes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Without the rights memo, defective service is just bad mailing with feelings.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The case is ugly, but its bones are visible. That is enough for a first hearing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: District Court. Where confusion wears a robe and asks for submissions.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Start with service. If the door was never opened, the room was never entered.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Good. The address problem has reached the bench without drowning.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The envelope looked guilty is not a submission. It is weather inside a sentence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Now say why it matters. Procedure is not the garnish; it is how the client gets heard.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: That landed. I almost made an approving face. Almost.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Correct idea, soft landing. Next time use the memo before the vibes arrive.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Ask for a reset, not a parade. Modest remedies survive contact with judges.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Good. You asked for the door to reopen, not the building to apologise.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The hearing is reset. The client stays. The firm continues its argument with extinction.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Messy, but alive. That describes most of our jurisprudence and this office.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Not bad. I have seen worse first days. Some of them were mine.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Mr. Pig is celebrating solvency. Solvency has not confirmed attendance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: A postcard from Swine. If it contains the words 'business opportunity', evacuate the ledger.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The landlord alleges disturbance. A poster is usually quiet. Suspicious discrepancy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Eviction over a poster is either disproportionate or missing several facts. Possibly both. We should be greedy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Severe consequence, vague allegations, no dates. The notice is wearing a suit and no shoes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Home, rule, facts, proportion. Four hooks. Finally, a wall that admits it has nails.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Residential District. More windows, fewer marble excuses.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Look at the balconies. Flags, flowers, signs. If only one poster caused civilisation to tremble, find out why.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: 'Visual agitation.' A phrase written by someone frightened of curtains.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The mailboxes are more specific than the eviction notice. That is rarely a good sign.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Small room. Serious consequence. Landlords often forget that ratio.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: This is the dangerous object? I have seen staplers with more coup potential.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Photograph it. The court should see the alleged emergency before it becomes legend.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The letters become sterner and less specific. A classic bureaucratic moult.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Visible from the street, yes. Destructive, no. The distinction may startle management.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: You do not need to apologise for having a window.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Now we need witnesses. If the poster disturbed anyone, someone will be delighted to complain.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: That door has opinions. Possibly minutes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: He complains with timestamps. Annoying, but evidentially promising.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Let him finish. Somewhere between soup, violin, and pigeons there may be a fact.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Good. If Kowalski heard nothing, the poster was either silent or legally supernatural. I choose silent.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Repeated photographs before eviction. That sounds less like complaint response and more like file manufacture.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: She watches people, not paperwork. We need both kinds of truth.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Other decorations ignored, Waldek threatened. Selective enforcement is not a mood; it is a problem.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: A complaint page landed in soup. The file has already shown better judgment than management.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: We have no disturbance and possible targeting. Now get the rule they claim did all this work.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: This room has been disinfected of empathy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The anti-poster sign is a poster. I assume irony has been evicted.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Pre-approved invisible expression. Bold theory. Cowardly wallpaper.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: 'As determined by management.' That is not a standard. That is a mirror with a stamp.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Grzyb has arrived. The room is now legally beige.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: He calls it order when his client defines the rule after choosing the target.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Dates. Look for dates. Vague outrage is cheap; chronology costs extra.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The complaint record is escaping. Somehow not the weakest thing it has done today.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Stamp pages with dates, names, and complaint text. Avoid anything that looks like lunch, decoration, or landlord poetry.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Dates after the notice. Excellent. The complaint history has tripped over its own shoelaces.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Damaged, but enough. The record limps. We can cross-examine a limp.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The pages have become weather. Annoying. We still have witnesses, and witnesses rarely blow into drains.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The landlord's file is worse than advertised. I appreciate honest incompetence from documents.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Do not arrange the board by emotional intensity. Notice, rule, facts, proportionality.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Good. The notice proves the allegation was vague before it became severe.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The rule belongs there. It is overbroad enough to cover weather.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: No real disturbance. The landlord has a complaint-shaped gap.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Poster against eviction. Small act, massive consequence. That is the ratio the court needs.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: That document matters, just not there. A hammer is not wrong because it fails as soup.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Good board. Almost alarming. We may appear deliberate.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: We can proceed. The argument is standing, but it should not be asked to run.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Housing Court. Where small rooms decide whether people keep smaller rooms.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Attack the rule. If it means anything management dislikes, it means nothing useful.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Good. Beige has been asked to define itself.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Now the complaints. Weak record, strong consequence. Courts dislike that exchange rate.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Use the dated page. Nothing says 'manufactured concern' like chronology arriving late.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Witness route, then. Less paper, more stairs, still usable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Do not argue that posters are sacred. Argue that eviction is heavy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: That is the case. Harmless expression, home consequence, no real harm. Tidy enough to hurt.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Grzyb is offended by facts being placed near his rule. Promising.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Eviction stopped. Poster survived. Management may need a chair.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The court noticed the rule, the target, and the missing disturbance. A rare three-course meal.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Temporary safety. Not elegant, but neither is eviction over stationery.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The poster can stay. Try not to let it develop additional legal personality.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: There are three posters now. Rights have reproduced.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The printer made a sound after the judgment. I recommend not forgiving it in advance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: I refuse to blame the printer until we have ruled out people. People are cheaper to distrust.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Blank page, missing filing, active deadline. That is not a mood. That is a sequence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Deadline today. Conveniently brutal. Someone either got lucky or scheduled malice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: If the printer is sentient, it has terrible litigation strategy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Do not negotiate with office equipment until it has counsel.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Call Bajtek. Superstition has produced no logs.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Good. Someone who distrusts printers professionally.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: This room hums like evidence trying to avoid cross-examination.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: This cable is cleaner than the rest. In this office, cleanliness is probable cause.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Repeated redirection at the same time. Random failure does not keep appointments.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Backup drive. The closest thing this office has to a trustworthy adult.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: He says random. The log says scheduled. Introduce them.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: There. Not random. The printer has been framed by punctuality.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: That does not contradict him. It merely annoys the room.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Now find who touched maintenance. Devices do not acquire conspiracies without invoices.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Business District. Glass, suits, and plausible deniability.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: These printers could evict a neighbourhood by lunch.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Our office printer is a kettle compared to this thing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: A rival firm paid for maintenance on our equipment. That is either fraud or an avant-garde gift.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Careful. Szpon speaks like a man who files knives alphabetically.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: No connection. Apart from the invoice, date, model, and signature. Sparse, but we manage.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: That touched a nerve. Nerves are often attached to facts.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: He denied connection. Present connection, not office folklore.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Logs show redirection. Invoice shows access. Now find the physical trick.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Basement. Where old files go to become motive.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Box marked 'Urgent 2014'. I admire its patience.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Follow the clean cable. Dust is often the honest witness.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: There. Small device, large misconduct. Classic.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Logs, invoice, device. The printer has an alibi, which is embarrassing for everyone.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Do not apologise to the printer yet. We still need to survive court.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Electronic filing is compromised. Physical delivery is now legal technology.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Please keep the filing more intact than the traffic laws.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Pages first, pride second. Avoid trams; courts are unsympathetic to tram-based drafting.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Filing delivered with only moderate wind trauma. Excellent.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The filing has tyre marks. Still more legible than Szpon's denial.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Bad ride. Not fatal. The sabotage evidence now has to do more lifting.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Start with the pattern. Ordinary failure does not repeat itself at 02:13 like a clerk with a grudge.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The judge accepts abnormal. Good. Now make it attributable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Invoice, then device. Make Szpon carry both.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: He looks polished, except around the facts.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Ask the court to accept the filing and prevent bad faith from collecting interest.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Good. Deadline restored. Trapdoor closed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Wrong document. The court asked who caused the hole, not who disliked falling.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Filing accepted. Default avoided. The printer has been demoted from suspect to victim.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Szpon formally warned, filing accepted, printer exonerated. Busy morning.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Provisional acceptance. Not elegant, but neither is crawling out of a procedural pit.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: I apologise for suspecting you before suspecting rival counsel. That was unfair to machinery.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: It printed 'THANK YOU.' I am choosing not to develop feelings.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: And there it is. Emotional growth has a toner limit.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: A bill attached to Swine's postcard. That is not correspondence. That is foreshadowing with numbers.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: International arbitration papers. Swine has finally returned as a jurisdictional event.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: He is wearing ski goggles indoors. Innocence remains unproven.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: He signed many things. That is not a fact pattern. That is a weather warning.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Claim amount, parties, deadline. At least the disaster brought labels.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Seventeen puddings. I am not saying fraud, but accounting has developed a sweet tooth.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Let Pig shout. It may shake a fact loose.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Contract, authority, charges, translation. Four places for Swine to have made one problem worse.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Airports are good for timelines. Bad for dignity. Useful trade.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Arrival time conflicts with the invoice period. Either Swine skied through customs, or the bill is ambitious.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: No commercial intent. Ski socks, documents, ceremonial invoice. Strange, but helpful.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Swine's luggage has better privilege instincts than he does.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The clerk remembers the folder. Good. Paper leaves witnesses when people panic in public.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Snow, banner, clipboard. We have reached the evidential stage of a postcard.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Good. A room where words may finally mean one thing at a time.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Ms. Tanaka is precise. I am already less angry at the file.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: 'Enthusiastic cooperation in principle' is a long walk from 'pay us or perish.'
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Appointment log confirms timing. I enjoy a document that knows where it was.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The problem is not translation. The problem is Swine behaving like ambiguity was a hospitality package.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Now find the draft. If the scary clause appeared late, the claim starts shedding teeth.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: This apartment has more surface area than our solvency plan.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: These goggles have seen both snow and bad judgment.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The expenses are chaotic, but chaos has dates. Dates can be cross-examined.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: No binding payment clause here. The later version grew teeth in a different font.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Transfer to an unpaid rights case. Swine may be reckless, but not randomly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: He dodged that too quickly. Evasion has a rhythm; his just picked up tempo.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: We have documents. Now we need his memory, assuming it survived the slope.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Recover the memory fragments. Avoid trees, consultants, and whatever Swine considered networking.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: He remembers the welcome memorandum. Ceremonial, not commercial. Miracles do occur downhill.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Clipboard, toast, fear. Not elegant, but enough to locate the lie.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The memory is mostly snow and pudding. We will lean on documents, as civilisation intended.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Cula handles law. Swine handles facts, preferably the ones that still have receipts.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Use the draft and translation note. Do not let Swine explain consideration through hospitality feelings.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Authority belongs to Cula. Swine had authority over skis and apparently dessert.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Swine can explain the puddings. This may be his first narrow area of expertise.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Document first. Courtesy is not a payment clause, no matter how expensive the stationery.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The arbitrator sees the gap between polite and binding. A useful canyon.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: No firm approval, no clear mandate, no assumption of liability. Travel is not authority.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Swine leading on authority is how we arrived here emotionally.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Now let Swine be useful. If he cannot explain seventeen puddings, no one can.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Two puddings admitted, seventeen rejected. That is the narrowest dignity we could hope for.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Separate Swine's judgment from the firm's liability. The tribunal should punish neither arithmetic nor architecture.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Firm liability collapses. Swine's personal dessert exposure remains under observation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Claim dismissed against the firm. Translation, draft, invoice. Three documents did what Swine could not.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The firm is safe. Swine may owe something personal, which is legally and emotionally efficient.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Major liability gone, minor accounting remains. The case survived Swine. Not all witnesses can say that.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Some of his money trail does not match luxury travel. It matches unpaid cases.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: He is still hiding something. Annoyingly, it may be decent.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Citywide notice. Same smell as every bad file: generic language, specific harm.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Hundreds of notices, same phrasing, different lives. Efficient, in the way a guillotine is efficient.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Pig & Swine must prove social utility. I assume panic is not accepted under that heading.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The ship metaphor has returned. Unfortunately, so has the water.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Mass notice, individual harm, technical filter, private benefit. Finally, a conspiracy large enough for its paperwork.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Start with impact. Generic notices hurt specific people. Courts need names.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: This is what the notice template tried not to describe.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Good. Tenants, clients, small firms. Consequences in handwriting.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Waldek kept his home. Now the template has returned with friends.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Kowalski read the notice six times and appealed himself. That is almost a legal remedy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Zielinska is right. Treating different cases the same is not fairness. It is a photocopier with robes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Even the café is noticed. Apparently caffeine access is being modernised into absence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Lose the archive, lose the cases people forgot how to prove. That matters.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Now compare the people to the plan. Generic harm usually has a committee appendix.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: City Hall. Polished floors, evasive verbs.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: It promises an access-to-justice hub while removing people providing access to justice. Ambitious sinkhole.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Identical notices, different legal situations. Individual assessment has been replaced by stationery.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: There. The contractor profits when the noticed people disappear. Paper leads to money. It usually does.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: He says individual assessment. The batch says copy-paste with a necktie.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Then every individual apparently had the same margins, syntax, and printer settings.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Use the template batch. We are contradicting individual assessment, not interior design.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Public interest is not a solvent. It does not dissolve people.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: We have motive and method on paper. Now find the machine that made the paper.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: That printer could bury a borough before lunch.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: One command, hundreds of consequences. Automation has excellent handwriting and no conscience.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Objections marked duplicate before review. The system did not lose them. It disliked them efficiently.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Bajtek found the folder. Its name is worse than I expected, which is difficult.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Szpon is here. Of course he is. Predators follow paper trails too.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The printer is weaponising notice. I withdraw all earlier jokes about stationery.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Dodge the duplicates, collect objections, stamp the emergency filings. Try not to become an exhibit.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Clean shutdown, objections recovered. The machine has lost its appetite.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Messy shutdown. Enough logs survived. The objections look traumatised but admissible.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Emergency shutdown. Fewer objections, same ugly architecture. We can still prove the scheme.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Swine labelled pro-bono payments as ski expenses. I hate that this is generous.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: He hid charity inside nonsense. That is either noble or tax-resistant. Possibly both.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Pig is learning the firm was saved by behaviour resembling its destruction. Difficult lesson.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Seven points, limited people. Assign witnesses to what they know, not what would be funny.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Defective notice needs Murrow or Kowalski. One structures the law, the other demonstrates the confusion by existing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Impact belongs to Waldek or Zielinska. People who received the blow describe it best.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Technical suppression is Bajtek. Do not assign Whimsy unless the court requests dragon-based algorithms.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Conflict of interest is mine or Crab's lane. Follow the paper until it asks to be paid.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Proportionality can take Whimsy, if supervised. His metaphors are dangerous but occasionally load-bearing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Social utility needs Pig and Swine. Astonishing sentence. Still true.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Closing argument is Cula. The rest of us provide ammunition, not fireworks.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The brief has structure, facts, and witnesses in their proper cages. Good.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Viable, but thin. The brief breathes through one nostril.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Supreme Court. Everyone speak less than usual. Especially the furniture.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Show the notice batch. A notice no ordinary person can act on is decoration with consequences.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The Court sees the template problem. Good. The photocopier has entered evidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Now impact. Public interest cannot flatten individual lives and call the surface smooth.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: That landed. The plan has met a person and looks smaller.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Use the filter log. Neutral systems do not hide folders called not helpful.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Automation did not remove discretion. It concealed it. The Court heard that.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Procurement link now. Same people print the notices and profit from the exits.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The money trail has entered the room. It dislikes the lighting.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Now the ledger. Pig & Swine is ridiculous, but it kept cases alive. That is not branding. That is evidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The Court understands the door. That may save the building.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Modernisation, efficiency, disappearance. He has arranged the words from cleanest to ugliest.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Wrong person for the point. The fact may be true, but it arrived in the wrong shoes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Scheme suspended, objections restored, procurement reviewed. Efficient, but in the correct direction.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The district survives pending lawful review. Not a revolution. Better: a remedy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Temporary injunction. Thin, but breathing. This firm can work with breathing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The office is still shabby. Good. Sudden respectability would have been suspicious.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Bajtek labelled the firewall 'do not negotiate.' Finally, office policy I support.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: Grzyb sent a beige congratulatory card. It may be the strongest emotion he owns.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: The printer says welcome back. I am not ready for reconciliation with office equipment.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
text: People came through the door because the polished doors were closed. That is a better argument than our sign deserves.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
context: Beat 5 first approach before binder is in the room
text: The binder is not in the room. I cannot check service without the binder.
notes: Phase 7 V1.2 draft; refusal refers to work only, not motivation; not final committed game dialogue.

speaker: Crab
context: Beat 5 second approach with binder on the table
text: The binder cites Article 132. The notice mentions service. I can check that.
notes: Phase 7 V1.2 draft; professional engagement, not enthusiasm; not final committed game dialogue.

speaker: Crab
context: Beat 5 V1.2 pass 3 — two-word acknowledgment of Cula's existence
text: Cula. Heard.
notes: Phase 7 V1.2 pass 3 draft; minimal social acknowledgment before the canonical refusal; not final.

speaker: Crab
context: Beat 5 V1.2 pass 3 — first refusal: binder is not in the room
text: The binder is not in the room. I cannot check service without the binder.
notes: Phase 7 V1.2 pass 3 draft; refusal refers to the work, not stance; not final.

speaker: Crab
context: Beat 5 V1.2 pass 3 — engagement after binder secured
text: The binder cites Article 132. The notice mentions service. I can check that.
notes: Phase 7 V1.2 pass 3 draft; efficient and professional; not final.

speaker: Crab
context: Beat 8 opening; brief courteous acknowledgment of Halina (one syllable past will-not-say floor)
text: Mrs. Sikorska.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
context: Beat 8 sympathetic carrier; scenting-leverage on Wójcik witness — case-tractable, not sympathetic
text: The neighbour's statement. Continuous resident, second floor, since nineteen ninety-one. We can take that.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
context: Beat 8 blunt-procedural carrier; scenting-leverage on slip — pure procedural
text: The return-to-sender slip. If it is in his file, it is producible by request. The slip alone is enough on knowledge.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
context: Beat 8 technical carrier; chain-and-defect summary — bible's two-sentence rhythm honoured (compressed from pass-1 five-sentence overload); 'ends the landlord's case' cooler than pass-2 'kills the landlord' for client room
text: The chain is clean through both reorganisations. The procedural defect ends the landlord's case before he reaches the merits.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Crab
context: Beat 5 V1.2 pass 6 — engagement after binder secured; V1.4-alignment-confirmed (Article 132 service citation per division of labor)
text: The binder cites Article 132. The notice mentions service. I can check that.
notes: Phase 7 V1.2 pass 6 draft; text identical to crab_ch01_v1_2_p3_engage_001 (Article 132 was always correct); pass 4/5 over-correction to Article 135-bis § 2 reverted in pass 6 per V1.4 division of labor (Whimsy Beat 8: 'Article 135-bis carries the doctrine; Article 132 carries the service. The combination is the case.'; Beat 9 closing assigns Crab the 'service-and-knowledge appendix'); not final.

speaker: Crab
context: Beat 12 V1.6 pass 5 — Round 1 floor argument (sympathetic carrier; affidavit tendered separately by Cula)
text: Your Honour. The notice went to number seven. The petitioner's address has been number twelve since the renumbering of two thousand and fifteen. Article 132 sets the service requirement; Article 135-bis § 2 of the Code of Civil Procedure sets the consequence of failed service. The notice is invalid.
notes: Phase 7 V1.6 pass 5 draft; sympathetic carrier — clean floor; paired observation-implication rhythm; not final.

speaker: Crab
context: Beat 12 V1.6 pass 5 — Round 1 floor argument with slip and countersignature integrated (blunt-procedural carrier)
text: Your Honour. The notice went to number seven. The petitioner's address has been number twelve since the renumbering of two thousand and fifteen. The landlord's own file contains a returned letter to number seven and a countersigned renewal at number twelve. Knowledge is in the slip and on the document. Article 132 sets the service requirement; Article 135-bis § 2 of the Code of Civil Procedure sets the consequence of failed service. The notice is invalid.
notes: Phase 7 V1.6 pass 5 draft; blunt-procedural carrier — return-to-sender slip + countersignature surface as knowledge; not final.

speaker: Crab
context: Beat 12 V1.6 pass 5 — Round 1 floor argument with countersignature (technical carrier; lease + inheritance held for Round 3)
text: Your Honour. The notice went to number seven. The petitioner's address has been number twelve since the renumbering of two thousand and fifteen. The two thousand and nineteen renewal carries the corrected address with a countersignature from the landlord's office. Knowledge is on the document. Article 132 sets the service requirement; Article 135-bis § 2 of the Code of Civil Procedure sets the consequence of failed service. The notice is invalid.
notes: Phase 7 V1.6 pass 5 draft; technical carrier — countersignature does the knowledge work the slip would do; not final.

speaker: Crab
context: Beat 13 V1.7 pass 5 — professional-indifference office reaction; two-sentence rhythm; observation then implication; ties to V1.6 Round 1 ground argument
text: The renumbering was the ground. The order does what the ground required.
notes: Phase 7 V1.7 pass 5 commit shape; Crab held at this line per pass-4 arbitration (V1.6 spine-tie + Crab→Whimsy handoff); not final.
